author: w3Lb
content: WHY: sed's /begin/,/end/ addressing — 'only operate between these markers' — has no ripsed equivalent; -n N:M is numeric only. Real agent case: edit only inside the [dependencies] section of a TOML. SCOPE: ripsed-core — new RangeSpec enum { Lines(LineRange), Patterns { start: String, end: String, regex: bool } }; engine tracks in-range state per line (toggle on start-match, toggle off after end-match; multiple regions supported; unterminated end = through EOF, matching sed). CLI: extend -n to accept '/start/,/end/' syntax or add --range-pattern start end (decide in implementation; document precedence with -n). JSON: "line_range" stays, add "range": {"start_pattern": ..., "end_pattern": ...} validated mutually exclusive with line_range. TESTS: single region, multiple regions, nested start markers, unterminated region to EOF, start==end pattern on same line, interaction with delete (deleting the marker lines themselves).
